map|Nagoya|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=28.1|lon_deg=136|lon_min=56|lon_sec=36.3|label=鳴海八幡宮|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|4|28.1|N|136|56|36.3|E}}}} }} '''鳴海八幡宮'''（なるみはちまんぐう）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|緑区 (名古屋市)|Q1155209|緑区}}{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=8}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛知郡}}「{{jalink|川原神社|Q3194327}}」の論社とも「伊副神社」の論社ともされる{{Sfn|愛知県郷土資料刊行会|1976|p=428}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=7}}。 === 概要 === 古くから「八幡」と呼ばれており、{{jalink|成海神社|Q11495515}}の別宮とされていた。神社の所在地は、{{jalink|江戸期}}に善之庵村と称されていた地域で、{{jalink|鳴海村}}の{{jalink|枝郷|Q11533553}}であった。創建年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の社職の記録があることや、境内に樹齢1000年を超える{{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}}が残ることから{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}以前に遡る古社の可能性が指摘されている。後述するように、式内社の論社となっている。 当神社周辺からは、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}の集落跡「前之輪遺跡」が見つかっており{{jalink|弥生土器|Q11487461}}などが出土している。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}（品陀和気命）{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}（息長帯日賣命）{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依毘賣命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|邇々藝命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月讀命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} === 歴史 === 当社は鎌倉時代には既に存在していたらしく、{{jalink|貞永|Q1194168}}元年（{{jalink|1232年|Q5418}}）に久野家初代の大賀元久なる人物が社職となったという記録が残っている。古くから'''八幡宮'''と称されており、鳴海東宮（成海神社）と対に扱われていた。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国期}}には{{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}や{{jalink|山口重政|Q11467507|山口長次郎重政}}が当社と鳴海東宮に{{jalink|社領}}を{{jalink|寄進|Q11455717}}した記録が残ってる。 『{{jalink|尾張徇行記}}』によれば、神官の久野氏が鳴海八幡宮が『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の尾張国愛知郡「川原神社」であるという書上を提出している。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』によれば、江戸期の釣燈籠や{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}には「河原神社」と書かれているものがあるという。 一方、式内社の尾張国愛知郡「伊副神社」にあたるという説もあるが{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|榊原|2020|pp=64}}{{Efn|{{jalink|郷土史家}}の榊原邦彦によると、伊副神社が平安時代後期に廃絶すると、熱田大宮司が八幡宮を勘請し、応永年中（{{jalink|1394年|Q6398}}～{{jalink|1428年|Q6315}}）には廃絶していた伊副神社の鎮座地を旧字地蔵山（{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}字小松山）から、旧字丹下（鳴海町字清水寺）に八幡宮として再興したという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|榊原|2012|pp=213}}。室町時代初期にその地から、{{jalink|扇川|Q11496839|黒末川}}のクスノキの大木がある河口の州であった現在の字前之輪の地に移転したという{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=88}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=5}}{{Sfn|榊原|2020|pp=64}}{{Sfn|榊原|1984|pp=103}}。伊副神社はその後八幡宮の末社北之社として祀られていたが、江戸時代に廃絶したという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=5}}。}}、『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』の記述から伊副神社は愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|日部郷}}にあったと推定され、当社を伊副神社に比定するには無理がある。 {{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}3年（{{jalink|1690年|Q7678}}）に鳴海神社の神官を牧野家とともに務めていた{{jalink|鳴海宿|Q3033344}}本町の久野家が前之輪に移住し、神官を兼任した{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に牧野家神官と久野家神官が口論し、祭礼が分裂。作町から中島までの氏子が成海神社の氏子から八幡宮の氏子に変わり、祭りも表方と裏方に分かれて行うようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。近年は、表と裏がひとつになり鳴海宿本町に山車が参集する取り組みが見られる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 === 年表 === * {{jalink|弘治 (日本)|Q1143910|弘治}}17年（{{jalink|1557年|Q6595}}）12月3日 - 今川義元が八幡宮と鳴海東宮（成海神社）へ神田11貫50文寄進{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。 * {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}17年（{{jalink|1589年|Q6759}}） - 山口長次郎重政が八幡宮と鳴海東宮両神社神主へ社領20貫文寄進した{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。 * {{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}8年（{{jalink|1622年|Q6815}}）8月 - 本殿を再建する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}。 * {{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}） - 牧野家神官と久野家神官が口論をした結果、作町から中島までの氏子が成海神社の氏子から八幡宮の氏子に変わり、祭りも表方と裏方に分かれて行うようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。 * {{jalink|文化|Q11042}}元年（{{jalink|1804年|Q6894}}） - 境内の樹木を使い手水舎を建造{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * {{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}2年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}） - 手水舎を再建{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 文政12年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}） - 拝殿や渡殿等を再建する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}18年（{{jalink|1733年|Q6972}}）11月1日 - {{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}主{{jalink|徳川宗春|Q6636871}}が鳴海八幡宮に参拝する{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=88}}。 * {{jalink|安永}}4年（{{jalink|1775年|Q7700}}）8月19日 - {{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}主{{jalink|徳川宗春|Q6636871}}が鳴海八幡宮に参拝する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=34}}。 * {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}2年（{{jalink|1737年|Q6993}}） - 御旅所を扇川畔に建立{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * {{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}6年（{{jalink|1859年|Q7687}}） - 鳥居を再建{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月28日 - {{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|村社}}に昇格。神社名を八幡社から八幡宮へ改める{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}。 * 明治13年（{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}） - 社殿を造営する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 *明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）10月26日 - 幣帛料供進社指定村社となる{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}。 * {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}6年（{{jalink|1931年|Q18782}}） - 社殿を造営する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 昭和9年（{{jalink|1934年|Q18714}}）4月 - 扇川畔の御旅所を新築する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和31年（{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}）7月 - 旧{{jalink|鳴海町|Q11675375}}役場庁舎を社務所として移築{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 昭和43年（{{jalink|1959年|Q5302}}） - {{jalink|伊勢湾台風|Q1060156}}により被害を受け、拝殿や手水舎など倒壊{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和43年（{{jalink|1968年|Q2644}}） - 拝殿や本殿、末社、手水舎を新築するとともに、鳴海八幡宮と改称{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和48年（{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}） - 末社稲荷社を相殿から別殿へ遷す{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}} * {{jalink|平成|Q234510}}12年（{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}） - 末社北野天満社を相殿から別殿へ遷す{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}} === 主な年中行事 === * 歳旦祭：正月{{jalink|元日|Q196627|元旦}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 北野天満社{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}：正月上旬{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|節分|Q746798}}祭：2月節分の日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|紀元節|Q11604982|紀元祭}}：2月11日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}：3月後半の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷}}祭：5月の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 輪くぐり神事：7月30日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 香良洲社例祭：8月12日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 例大祭（表方祭）：10月前半の土曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|七五三|Q1072387}}：11月前半の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}：11月23日頃{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 秋葉社例祭：12月16日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|天皇誕生日|Q72318|天長祭}}：12月23日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 除夜祭（{{jalink|大祓|Q11261910}}）：12月31日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 月次祭：毎月前半{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} === 境内 === === 神木 === クスノキの大木で、幹の太さは直径8メートルある{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}には既に大木であったと伝えられており、樹齢1000年～1200年と推定されている。平成12年（{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}）に愛知県樹木医会が治療を行った{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=23}}。 === 社務所 === {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}36年（{{jalink|1903年|Q2045}}）に{{jalink|鳴海町|Q11675375}}役場の庁舎として建てられ、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}31年（{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}）7月に現在の地へ社務所として移築した{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。名古屋市より認定地域建造物資産に認定されている{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}。 === 摂末社 === 現在は末社として次の10社がある{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=11}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}}。かつては、他になど5数社があったという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=13}}。 === 現在の摂末社 === * 稲荷社：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂}}大神{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=11}} * 高良社：{{jalink|建内宿禰}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 北野天満社：{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 秋葉社：{{jalink|軻遇突智}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 国造社：{{jalink|大国主神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 須佐之男社：{{jalink|須佐之男}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 金刀比羅社：{{jalink|大物主神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 御鍬社：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}、{{jalink|豊受大神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 香良洲社：{{jalink|稚日女尊|Q11596349|稚日女命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 祓戸社：{{jalink|瀬織津姫|Q13579836|瀬織津比咩}}神、{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} === 昔の摂末社 === * 北之社：{{jalink|高御産巣日神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 国造宮：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 道分社：{{jalink|猿田毘古神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 居森社：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日孁貴幸魂}}、{{jalink|須佐之男命||須佐之男命幸魂}}、{{jalink|疹神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} === ギャラリー === ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 02.jpg|鳥居 ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 04.jpg|北野天満社 ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 03.jpg|香良洲社 === 祭礼 === かつて祭礼は同じ鳴海に鎮座する{{jalink|成海神社|Q11495515}}と同日に町をあげて行われていたが、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に両神社のあいだで祭礼論争が起き、それ以来表方（鳴海八幡宮）と裏方（成海神社）に分かれて行われるようになった{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=34}}。近年は、表と裏がひとつになり鳴海宿本町に山車が参集する取り組みが見られ、祭礼が分裂する以前のように祭りが行われるようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 鳴海八幡宮例大祭（表方祭）は旧8月15日に行っていたものを、大正元年（{{jalink|1912年|Q2077}}）より{{jalink|9月15日|Q2846}}とし、昭和から{{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}}にした。近年では例祭の日を{{jalink|10月|Q124}}前半の土曜日としている{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}}。 猩々祭りでは{{jalink|猩々}}と呼ばれる赤い顔の面と上半身分の竹枠組みで出来たおりその上から衣装で覆った巨大人形が登場し、大人が猩々の人形をかぶり、子供達を追いかけ回す「鳴海八幡宮例大祭（表方祭）」がある。猩々とは鳴海八幡宮の神様である。猩々は作り物の手や棒で子供たちをたたき、猩々に叩かれた子供たちは病気にならないと伝わる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 === 交通 === * {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}} {{jalink|鳴海駅|Q6966454}}より徒歩で約20分。 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|大高駅|Q871424}}より徒歩で約20分。 {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭 = 10月前半の土曜日 |別名 = 八幡
鳴海神社

河原神社 |創建 = 不明（{{jalink|13世紀|Q7049}}以前か） |地図 = {{Location map|Nagoya|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=28.1|lon_deg=136|lon_min=56|lon_sec=36.3|label=鳴海八幡宮|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|4|28.1|N|136|56|36.3|E}}}} }} '''鳴海八幡宮'''（なるみはちまんぐう）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|緑区 (名古屋市)|Q1155209|緑区}}{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=8}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛知郡}}「{{jalink|川原神社|Q3194327}}」の論社とも「伊副神社」の論社ともされる{{Sfn|愛知県郷土資料刊行会|1976|p=428}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=7}}。 === 概要 === 古くから「八幡」と呼ばれており、{{jalink|成海神社|Q11495515}}の別宮とされていた。神社の所在地は、{{jalink|江戸期}}に善之庵村と称されていた地域で、{{jalink|鳴海村}}の{{jalink|枝郷|Q11533553}}であった。創建年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の社職の記録があることや、境内に樹齢1000年を超える{{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}}が残ることから{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}以前に遡る古社の可能性が指摘されている。後述するように、式内社の論社となっている。 当神社周辺からは、{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}の集落跡「前之輪遺跡」が見つかっており{{jalink|弥生土器|Q11487461}}などが出土している。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}（品陀和気命）{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}（息長帯日賣命）{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依毘賣命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|邇々藝命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|ツクヨミ|Q595520|月讀命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=3}} === 歴史 === 当社は鎌倉時代には既に存在していたらしく、{{jalink|貞永|Q1194168}}元年（{{jalink|1232年|Q5418}}）に久野家初代の大賀元久なる人物が社職となったという記録が残っている。古くから'''八幡宮'''と称されており、鳴海東宮（成海神社）と対に扱われていた。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国期}}には{{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}や{{jalink|山口重政|Q11467507|山口長次郎重政}}が当社と鳴海東宮に{{jalink|社領}}を{{jalink|寄進|Q11455717}}した記録が残ってる。 『{{jalink|尾張徇行記}}』によれば、神官の久野氏が鳴海八幡宮が『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の尾張国愛知郡「川原神社」であるという書上を提出している。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』によれば、江戸期の釣燈籠や{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}には「河原神社」と書かれているものがあるという。 一方、式内社の尾張国愛知郡「伊副神社」にあたるという説もあるが{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|榊原|2020|pp=64}}{{Efn|{{jalink|郷土史家}}の榊原邦彦によると、伊副神社が平安時代後期に廃絶すると、熱田大宮司が八幡宮を勘請し、応永年中（{{jalink|1394年|Q6398}}～{{jalink|1428年|Q6315}}）には廃絶していた伊副神社の鎮座地を旧字地蔵山（{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}字小松山）から、旧字丹下（鳴海町字清水寺）に八幡宮として再興したという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|榊原|2012|pp=213}}。室町時代初期にその地から、{{jalink|扇川|Q11496839|黒末川}}のクスノキの大木がある河口の州であった現在の字前之輪の地に移転したという{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=88}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=5}}{{Sfn|榊原|2020|pp=64}}{{Sfn|榊原|1984|pp=103}}。伊副神社はその後八幡宮の末社北之社として祀られていたが、江戸時代に廃絶したという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=5}}。}}、『{{jalink|尾張国風土記|Q135598023}}』の記述から伊副神社は愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|日部郷}}にあったと推定され、当社を伊副神社に比定するには無理がある。 {{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}3年（{{jalink|1690年|Q7678}}）に鳴海神社の神官を牧野家とともに務めていた{{jalink|鳴海宿|Q3033344}}本町の久野家が前之輪に移住し、神官を兼任した{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に牧野家神官と久野家神官が口論し、祭礼が分裂。作町から中島までの氏子が成海神社の氏子から八幡宮の氏子に変わり、祭りも表方と裏方に分かれて行うようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。近年は、表と裏がひとつになり鳴海宿本町に山車が参集する取り組みが見られる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 === 年表 === * {{jalink|弘治 (日本)|Q1143910|弘治}}17年（{{jalink|1557年|Q6595}}）12月3日 - 今川義元が八幡宮と鳴海東宮（成海神社）へ神田11貫50文寄進{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。 * {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}17年（{{jalink|1589年|Q6759}}） - 山口長次郎重政が八幡宮と鳴海東宮両神社神主へ社領20貫文寄進した{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。 * {{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}8年（{{jalink|1622年|Q6815}}）8月 - 本殿を再建する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}。 * {{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}） - 牧野家神官と久野家神官が口論をした結果、作町から中島までの氏子が成海神社の氏子から八幡宮の氏子に変わり、祭りも表方と裏方に分かれて行うようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=48}}。 * {{jalink|文化|Q11042}}元年（{{jalink|1804年|Q6894}}） - 境内の樹木を使い手水舎を建造{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * {{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}2年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}） - 手水舎を再建{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 文政12年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}） - 拝殿や渡殿等を再建する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * {{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}18年（{{jalink|1733年|Q6972}}）11月1日 - {{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}主{{jalink|徳川宗春|Q6636871}}が鳴海八幡宮に参拝する{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=88}}。 * {{jalink|安永}}4年（{{jalink|1775年|Q7700}}）8月19日 - {{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}主{{jalink|徳川宗春|Q6636871}}が鳴海八幡宮に参拝する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=34}}。 * {{jalink|元文|Q1145754}}2年（{{jalink|1737年|Q6993}}） - 御旅所を扇川畔に建立{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * {{jalink|安政|Q1327259}}6年（{{jalink|1859年|Q7687}}） - 鳥居を再建{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月28日 - {{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|村社}}に昇格。神社名を八幡社から八幡宮へ改める{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}。 * 明治13年（{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}） - 社殿を造営する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 *明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）10月26日 - 幣帛料供進社指定村社となる{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=4}}。 * {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}6年（{{jalink|1931年|Q18782}}） - 社殿を造営する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 昭和9年（{{jalink|1934年|Q18714}}）4月 - 扇川畔の御旅所を新築する{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和31年（{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}）7月 - 旧{{jalink|鳴海町|Q11675375}}役場庁舎を社務所として移築{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。 * 昭和43年（{{jalink|1959年|Q5302}}） - {{jalink|伊勢湾台風|Q1060156}}により被害を受け、拝殿や手水舎など倒壊{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和43年（{{jalink|1968年|Q2644}}） - 拝殿や本殿、末社、手水舎を新築するとともに、鳴海八幡宮と改称{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=15}}。 * 昭和48年（{{jalink|1973年|Q2477}}） - 末社稲荷社を相殿から別殿へ遷す{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}} * {{jalink|平成|Q234510}}12年（{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}） - 末社北野天満社を相殿から別殿へ遷す{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}} === 主な年中行事 === * 歳旦祭：正月{{jalink|元日|Q196627|元旦}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 北野天満社{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}：正月上旬{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|節分|Q746798}}祭：2月節分の日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|紀元節|Q11604982|紀元祭}}：2月11日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}：3月後半の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷}}祭：5月の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 輪くぐり神事：7月30日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 香良洲社例祭：8月12日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 例大祭（表方祭）：10月前半の土曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|七五三|Q1072387}}：11月前半の日曜日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}：11月23日頃{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 秋葉社例祭：12月16日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|天皇誕生日|Q72318|天長祭}}：12月23日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 除夜祭（{{jalink|大祓|Q11261910}}）：12月31日{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} * 月次祭：毎月前半{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=25}} === 境内 === === 神木 === クスノキの大木で、幹の太さは直径8メートルある{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=47}}。{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}には既に大木であったと伝えられており、樹齢1000年～1200年と推定されている。平成12年（{{jalink|2000年|Q1985}}）に愛知県樹木医会が治療を行った{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=23}}。 === 社務所 === {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}36年（{{jalink|1903年|Q2045}}）に{{jalink|鳴海町|Q11675375}}役場の庁舎として建てられ、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}31年（{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}）7月に現在の地へ社務所として移築した{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=14}}。名古屋市より認定地域建造物資産に認定されている{{Sfn|榊原|2000|pp=89}}。 === 摂末社 === 現在は末社として次の10社がある{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=11}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}}。かつては、他になど5数社があったという{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=13}}。 === 現在の摂末社 === * 稲荷社：{{jalink|宇迦之御魂}}大神{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=11}} * 高良社：{{jalink|建内宿禰}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 北野天満社：{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 秋葉社：{{jalink|軻遇突智}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 国造社：{{jalink|大国主神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 須佐之男社：{{jalink|須佐之男}}命{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 金刀比羅社：{{jalink|大物主神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 御鍬社：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}、{{jalink|豊受大神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 香良洲社：{{jalink|稚日女尊|Q11596349|稚日女命}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 祓戸社：{{jalink|瀬織津姫|Q13579836|瀬織津比咩}}神、{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} === 昔の摂末社 === * 北之社：{{jalink|高御産巣日神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 国造宮：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 道分社：{{jalink|猿田毘古神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} * 居森社：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日孁貴幸魂}}、{{jalink|須佐之男命||須佐之男命幸魂}}、{{jalink|疹神}}{{Sfn|榊原|2004|pp=12}} === ギャラリー === ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 02.jpg|鳥居 ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 04.jpg|北野天満社 ファイル:Narumi Yahata Shrine 03.jpg|香良洲社 === 祭礼 === かつて祭礼は同じ鳴海に鎮座する{{jalink|成海神社|Q11495515}}と同日に町をあげて行われていたが、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に両神社のあいだで祭礼論争が起き、それ以来表方（鳴海八幡宮）と裏方（成海神社）に分かれて行われるようになった{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=34}}。近年は、表と裏がひとつになり鳴海宿本町に山車が参集する取り組みが見られ、祭礼が分裂する以前のように祭りが行われるようになる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 鳴海八幡宮例大祭（表方祭）は旧8月15日に行っていたものを、大正元年（{{jalink|1912年|Q2077}}）より{{jalink|9月15日|Q2846}}とし、昭和から{{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}}にした。近年では例祭の日を{{jalink|10月|Q124}}前半の土曜日としている{{Sfn|榊原|2005|pp=26}}。 猩々祭りでは{{jalink|猩々}}と呼ばれる赤い顔の面と上半身分の竹枠組みで出来たおりその上から衣装で覆った巨大人形が登場し、大人が猩々の人形をかぶり、子供達を追いかけ回す「鳴海八幡宮例大祭（表方祭）」がある。猩々とは鳴海八幡宮の神様である。猩々は作り物の手や棒で子供たちをたたき、猩々に叩かれた子供たちは病気にならないと伝わる{{Sfn|淡河|2013|pp=35}}。 === 交通 === * {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}} {{jalink|鳴海駅|Q6966454}}より徒歩で約20分。 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|大高駅|Q871424}}より徒歩で約20分。 Narumi Shrine
Bettemiya
Kawahara Shrine |Foundation = unknown (before {{jalink|13th century|Q7049}})|Map = {{Location map|Nagoya|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=28.1|lon_deg=136|lon_min=56|lon_sec=36.3|label=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|4|28.1|N|136|56|36.3|E}}}} }}'''Narumi Hachiman Shrine'' (Narumi Hachimangu) is {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya City|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1155209|Midori Ward}}{{jalink|Narumi Town {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=8}} located in (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Narumi Town}}. {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture)|Q552687|Aichi District}} It is also said to be the Ronsha of ``{{jalink|Kawahara Shrine|Q3194327}}'' and the ronsha of ``Izoe Shrine.'' === Overview === It has been called "Hachiman" since ancient times, and was considered to be a separate shrine to {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}. The location of the shrine was {{jalink|Edago|Q11533553}} in {{jalink|Narumi Village}}, in the area that was called Zennoan Village in the {{jalink|Edo period}}. The year of its founding is unknown, but there are records of shrine occupations dating back to the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, and a {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}} that is over 1,000 years old remains within the precincts, suggesting that it may be an old shrine that dates back to before the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. As mentioned later, it is Shikinaisha's Ronsha. Around this shrine, the remains of a village from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}, ``Maenowa Ruins,'' have been discovered, and {{jalink|Yayoi pottery|Q11487461}} have been unearthed. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} (Shinada Wake no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} (Senagatai Hibi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayoribibimei}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Nishi Geishi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukiyomi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} === History === The company seems to have already existed in the Kamakura period, and there is a record that Motohisa Oga, the first generation member of the Kuno family, became a company official in the first year of {{jalink|Sadanaga|Q1194168}} ({{jalink|1232|Q5418}}). Since ancient times, it has been called ``Hachimangu Shrine'' and was treated as a companion to Narumi Togu Shrine (Narumi Shrine). {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|During the Warring States period}}, there is a record that {{jalink|Yoshimoto Imagawa|Q1054305}} and {{jalink|Shigemasa Yamaguchi|Q11467507|Chōjiro Shigemasa}} {{jalink|corporate territory}} was {{jalink|donated|Q11455717}} to our company and Narumi Togu Shrine. According to ``{{jalink|Owari Makiyōki}},'' the priest Mr. Kuno submitted a document stating that Narumi Hachimangu Shrine is ``Kawahara Shrine,'' Aichi District, Owari Province, in ``{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}.'' According to ``{{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092}}'', there are some Edo-era fishing lanterns and {{jalink|ridge tags|Q8008834}} that have the words ``Kawahara Shrine'' written on them.On the other hand, there is a theory that it corresponds to "Izoe Shrine" in Aichi District, Owari Province, but according to Kunihiko Sakakibara, a {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Efn|{{jalink|local historian}}, Izoe Shrine was built during the Heian period. When it was abolished in the late period, Atsuta Daiguji disowned Hachimangu Shrine, and during the Oei era ({{jalink|1394|Q6398}} to {{jalink|1428|Q6315}}), the enshrinement site of Izoe Shrine, which had been abolished, was renamed to the old Jizoyama ({{jalink|Narumi Town) (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Q136913130|Q136913130|Komatsuyama, Narumi Town), it is said to have been rebuilt as a Hachimangu Shrine in the former Tange (Kiyomizu Temple, Narumi Town). It is said that in the early Muromachi period, it was moved from that place to the present-day Azazenowa area, which was at the mouth of the {{jalink|Ogigawa|Q11496839|Kurosuegawa}} large camphor tree. n|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|1984|pp=103}}. Izoe Shrine was later enshrined as Kitanosha, a subsidiary shrine of Hachimangu Shrine, but it is said to have been abolished in the Edo period {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}. }}, from the description in ``{{jalink|Owari Province Fudoki|Q135598023}}'', it is presumed that Izoe Shrine was located in Aichi District {{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture) #Go|Q552687|Hibego}}, and it is unreasonable to compare our company with Izoe Shrine.In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}3 ({{jalink|1690|Q7678}}), he served as a priest at Narumi Shrine along with the Makino family.{{jalink| Narumi-juku|Q3033344}}The Kuno family of Honmachi moved to Maenowa and served as a priest.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}), the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests quarreled and the festival was divided. The parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In recent years, efforts have been seen to bring together the front and back sides of the festival and gather floats at Narumi-juku Honmachi.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} === Chronology === * {{jalink|Koji (Japan)|Q1143910|Koji}} December 3, 17 ({{jalink|1557|Q6595}}) - Yoshimoto Imagawa donates Kanda 11 kan 50 mon to Hachiman Shrine and Narumi Togu (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 17th year ({{jalink|1589|Q6759}}) - Chojiro Shigemasa Yamaguchi donated 20 kanmon of shrine territory to the priests of both Hachimangu and Narumi Togu shrines.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} August 8 ({{jalink|1622|Q6815}}) - Reconstruction of the main shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}) - As a result of an argument between the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests, the parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. * {{jalink|Culture|Q11042}} 1st year ({{jalink|1804|Q6894}}) - Constructed a water fountain using trees in the temple grounds {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2nd year ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the Chozuya.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}.* Bunsei 12 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the worship hall, watariden, etc.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}18 ({{jalink|1733|Q6972}}) November 1st - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} Lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachimangu Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}. * August 19, 4th year of {{jalink|Yasunaga}} ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachiman Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=34}}. * {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}2nd year ({{jalink|1737|Q6993}}) - Otabisho was built on the banks of the Ogi River{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6 ({{jalink|1859|Q7687}}) - Torii was rebuilt{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 28, {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - Promoted to {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Village company}}. The name of the shrine was changed from Hachiman-sha to Hachiman-gu {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}.* Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}) - Construction of the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. *October 26, 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}) - Became a village shrine designated by Heihakuryokushinsha {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}6 ({{jalink|1931|Q18782}}) - Constructing the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * April 1934 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}) - Built a new inn on the banks of the Ogi River.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 1956 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}) - The former {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building was relocated as a company office {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * 1969 ({{jalink|1959|Q5302}}) - Damaged by {{jalink|Isewan Typhoon|Q1060156}}, including the worship hall and water purification building.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * 1968 ({{jalink|1968|Q2644}}) - The worship hall, main hall, subsidiary shrine, and chozusha were newly built, and the name was changed to Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. * 1973 ({{jalink|1973|Q2477}}) - Transferred Suesha Inarisha from Aiden to Betsuden.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}* {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}12 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}) - Transferred the subsidiary shrine Kitano Tenmansha from the side hall to the separate hall{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}} === Main annual events === * Saitan Festival: New Year's Day {{jalink|New Year's Day|Q196627|New Year's Day}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Kitano Tenmansha {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}}: Early New Year {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival: February Setsubun Day {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Kigen Festival|Q11604982|Kigen Festival}}: February 11th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}: Sunday in the second half of March {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari}} Festival: Sunday in May {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Ring ritual: July 30th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Korazusha Annual Festival: August 12th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Reitaisai (Omote Festival): Saturday in the first half of October {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}}: Sunday in the first half of November {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}: Around November 23rd{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Akibasha Annual Festival: December 16th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Emperor's Birthday|Q72318|Tencho Festival}}: December 23rd {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}}* New Year's Eve Festival ({{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}}): December 31st {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Monthly Festival: First half of every month {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} === Precincts === === Sacred tree === It is a large camphor tree with a trunk 8 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. It is said that it was already a large tree during the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}, and is estimated to be 1000 to 1200 years old. In 2000 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}), the Aichi Prefectural Arborist Association conducted treatment{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=23}}. === Office === It was built in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}36 ({{jalink|1903|Q2045}}) as the {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}} It was moved to its current location as a shrine office in July of 1931 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}). It has been certified as a certified regional building asset by Nagoya City {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}. === Setsusha === Currently, there are the following ten subsidiary companies: {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}}. In the past, there were about five other companies. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Current Sessuesha === * Inari Shrine: {{jalink|Uka no Mitama}} Okami{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}} * Korasha: {{jalink|Kenuchi Sukune}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kitano Tenmansha: {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Akibasha: {{jalink|Kikunshuchi}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzosha: {{jalink|Okuninushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Susanoosha: {{jalink|Susanoo}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kotohira Shrine: {{jalink|Oimono Nushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Okuwa Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Toyouke Omikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Korazusha: {{jalink|Chikaji Onna no Mikoto|Q11596349|Chikkana no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Haraido Shrine: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|Seoritsuhime}} God, {{jalink|Kebukito Lord God|Q86734190}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Old Setsuesha === * Kitanosha: {{jalink|Takamisuhigami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzogu: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Doubunsha: {{jalink|Sarutabikojin}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Imori Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Dainichikai Takayuki Spirit}}, {{jalink|Susanoo Mikoto||Susanoo Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Isagami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Gallery === File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 02.jpg|Torii File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 04.jpg|Kitano Tenmansha File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 03.jpg|Korasusha === Festival === In the past, the festival was held throughout the town on the same day as {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}, which is also located in Narumi, but in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink |1700|Q6813}}), a dispute arose between the two shrines, and since then the festival has been divided into front (Narumi Hachiman Shrine) and back (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=34}}. In recent years, there have been efforts to unite the front and back sides and gather floats in Narumi-juku Honmachi, and the festival is now being held as before the festival was divided.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} The Narumi Hachiman Shrine annual festival (Omote Festival) used to be held on August 15th, but since the first year of the Taisho era ({{jalink|1912|Q2077}}) it has been held on {{jalink|September 15th|Q2846}}, and since the Showa era it has been held on {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}. In recent years, the annual festival day has been set on a Saturday in the first half of {{jalink|October|Q124}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}. At the Shōjo Festival, a giant doll called {{jalink|shōjō}} made of a bamboo framework with a red face mask and upper half of the body covered with a costume appears, and there is the ``Narumi Hachiman Shrine Reitaisai Festival'' (Omote Festival), where adults wear shōjō dolls and chase children around. Shōto is the deity of Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. The monkeys hit children with fake hands and sticks, and it is said that children who are hit by the monkeys do not get sick.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}}. === Transportation === * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Nagoya Railway|Q30850|Meitetsu}}{{jalink|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Nagoya Main Line}} {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}}. * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Tokai Railway Company|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|Tokaido Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|Tokaido Main Line}} {{jalink|Odaka Station|Q871424}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=1984|date=1984-11|chapter=|title=History of Midori Ward|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2004|date=2004-03|chapter=|title=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2004}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2005|date=2005-12|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Shrine Journal|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2012|date=2012-05|chapter=|title=Midori Ward local history|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2012}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Toshiyuki Awakawa|year=2013|date=2013-11|chapter=|title=Visiting the history of Midori|publisher=Midori Little History Museum|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Awakawa|2013}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2020|date=2020-08|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Local History|publisher=Narumi Tofukai|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2020}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2021|date=2021-10|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Historic Site Tour|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2021}}}} === External link === * {{Wayback|url=http://alay.at.webry.info/200610/article_26.html |title=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine |date=20090131165332}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20070930230314/http://www.ne.jp/asahi/no/va/owari/naru_omo/ Narumi Omote Festival (Narumi Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival)] {{shinto-stub}} Nagoya|uk|Район Мідорі (Наґоя)|ur|میدوری وارڈ، ناگویا|vi|Midori, Nagoya|zh|綠區 (名古屋市)|WD=Q1155209}} * {{ill|Q514506|lt=Q514506|WD=Q514506}} * {{ill|Q552687|lt=Q552687|WD=Q552687}} == coordinate category|Category:Narumi Hachiman-gū}} [[JA:鳴海八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11675363}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Narumi Hachimangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 01.jpg|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya City|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1155209|Midori Ward}}{{jalink|Narumi Town (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Narumi Town}} Aza Maenowa 23 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|35|4|28|N|136|56|36|E|}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803|Empress Jingu}}
{{jalink|Tamayoribime (Hyuga myth)|Q3082751|Tamayori Bisho Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Niyōbi Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukiyomi Mikoto}} |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}Ronsha
{{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular festival = Saturday in the first half of October |Alternative name = Hachiman
Narumi Shrine
Bettemiya
Kawahara Shrine |Foundation = unknown (before {{jalink|13th century|Q7049}})|Map = {{Location map|Nagoya|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=28.1|lon_deg=136|lon_min=56|lon_sec=36.3|label=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|4|28.1|N|136|56|36.3|E}}}} }}'''Narumi Hachiman Shrine'' (Narumi Hachimangu) is {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya City|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1155209|Midori Ward}}{{jalink|Narumi Town {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=8}} located in (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Narumi Town}}. {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture)|Q552687|Aichi District}} It is also said to be the Ronsha of ``{{jalink|Kawahara Shrine|Q3194327}}'' and the ronsha of ``Izoe Shrine.'' === Overview === It has been called "Hachiman" since ancient times, and was considered to be a separate shrine to {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}. The location of the shrine was {{jalink|Edago|Q11533553}} in {{jalink|Narumi Village}}, in the area that was called Zennoan Village in the {{jalink|Edo period}}. The year of its founding is unknown, but there are records of shrine occupations dating back to the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, and a {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}} that is over 1,000 years old remains within the precincts, suggesting that it may be an old shrine that dates back to before the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. As mentioned later, it is Shikinaisha's Ronsha. Around this shrine, the remains of a village from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}, ``Maenowa Ruins,'' have been discovered, and {{jalink|Yayoi pottery|Q11487461}} have been unearthed. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} (Shinada Wake no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} (Senagatai Hibi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayoribibimei}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Nishi Geishi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukiyomi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} === History === The company seems to have already existed in the Kamakura period, and there is a record that Motohisa Oga, the first generation member of the Kuno family, became a company official in the first year of {{jalink|Sadanaga|Q1194168}} ({{jalink|1232|Q5418}}). Since ancient times, it has been called ``Hachimangu Shrine'' and was treated as a companion to Narumi Togu Shrine (Narumi Shrine). {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|During the Warring States period}}, there is a record that {{jalink|Yoshimoto Imagawa|Q1054305}} and {{jalink|Shigemasa Yamaguchi|Q11467507|Chōjiro Shigemasa}} {{jalink|corporate territory}} was {{jalink|donated|Q11455717}} to our company and Narumi Togu Shrine. According to ``{{jalink|Owari Makiyōki}},'' the priest Mr. Kuno submitted a document stating that Narumi Hachimangu Shrine is ``Kawahara Shrine,'' Aichi District, Owari Province, in ``{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}.'' According to ``{{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092}}'', there are some Edo-era fishing lanterns and {{jalink|ridge tags|Q8008834}} that have the words ``Kawahara Shrine'' written on them.On the other hand, there is a theory that it corresponds to "Izoe Shrine" in Aichi District, Owari Province, but according to Kunihiko Sakakibara, a {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Efn|{{jalink|local historian}}, Izoe Shrine was built during the Heian period. When it was abolished in the late period, Atsuta Daiguji disowned Hachimangu Shrine, and during the Oei era ({{jalink|1394|Q6398}} to {{jalink|1428|Q6315}}), the enshrinement site of Izoe Shrine, which had been abolished, was renamed to the old Jizoyama ({{jalink|Narumi Town) (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Q136913130|Q136913130|Komatsuyama, Narumi Town), it is said to have been rebuilt as a Hachimangu Shrine in the former Tange (Kiyomizu Temple, Narumi Town). It is said that in the early Muromachi period, it was moved from that place to the present-day Azazenowa area, which was at the mouth of the {{jalink|Ogigawa|Q11496839|Kurosuegawa}} large camphor tree. n|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|1984|pp=103}}. Izoe Shrine was later enshrined as Kitanosha, a subsidiary shrine of Hachimangu Shrine, but it is said to have been abolished in the Edo period {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}. }}, from the description in ``{{jalink|Owari Province Fudoki|Q135598023}}'', it is presumed that Izoe Shrine was located in Aichi District {{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture) #Go|Q552687|Hibego}}, and it is unreasonable to compare our company with Izoe Shrine.In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}3 ({{jalink|1690|Q7678}}), he served as a priest at Narumi Shrine along with the Makino family.{{jalink| Narumi-juku|Q3033344}}The Kuno family of Honmachi moved to Maenowa and served as a priest.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}), the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests quarreled and the festival was divided. The parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In recent years, efforts have been seen to bring together the front and back sides of the festival and gather floats at Narumi-juku Honmachi.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} === Chronology === * {{jalink|Koji (Japan)|Q1143910|Koji}} December 3, 17 ({{jalink|1557|Q6595}}) - Yoshimoto Imagawa donates Kanda 11 kan 50 mon to Hachiman Shrine and Narumi Togu (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 17th year ({{jalink|1589|Q6759}}) - Chojiro Shigemasa Yamaguchi donated 20 kanmon of shrine territory to the priests of both Hachimangu and Narumi Togu shrines.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} August 8 ({{jalink|1622|Q6815}}) - Reconstruction of the main shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}) - As a result of an argument between the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests, the parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. * {{jalink|Culture|Q11042}} 1st year ({{jalink|1804|Q6894}}) - Constructed a water fountain using trees in the temple grounds {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2nd year ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the Chozuya.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}.* Bunsei 12 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the worship hall, watariden, etc.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}18 ({{jalink|1733|Q6972}}) November 1st - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} Lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachimangu Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}. * August 19, 4th year of {{jalink|Yasunaga}} ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachiman Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=34}}. * {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}2nd year ({{jalink|1737|Q6993}}) - Otabisho was built on the banks of the Ogi River{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6 ({{jalink|1859|Q7687}}) - Torii was rebuilt{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 28, {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - Promoted to {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Village company}}. The name of the shrine was changed from Hachiman-sha to Hachiman-gu {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}.* Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}) - Construction of the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. *October 26, 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}) - Became a village shrine designated by Heihakuryokushinsha {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}6 ({{jalink|1931|Q18782}}) - Constructing the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * April 1934 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}) - Built a new inn on the banks of the Ogi River.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 1956 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}) - The former {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building was relocated as a company office {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * 1969 ({{jalink|1959|Q5302}}) - Damaged by {{jalink|Isewan Typhoon|Q1060156}}, including the worship hall and water purification building.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * 1968 ({{jalink|1968|Q2644}}) - The worship hall, main hall, subsidiary shrine, and chozusha were newly built, and the name was changed to Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. * 1973 ({{jalink|1973|Q2477}}) - Transferred Suesha Inarisha from Aiden to Betsuden.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}* {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}12 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}) - Transferred the subsidiary shrine Kitano Tenmansha from the side hall to the separate hall{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}} === Main annual events === * Saitan Festival: New Year's Day {{jalink|New Year's Day|Q196627|New Year's Day}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Kitano Tenmansha {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}}: Early New Year {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival: February Setsubun Day {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Kigen Festival|Q11604982|Kigen Festival}}: February 11th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}: Sunday in the second half of March {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari}} Festival: Sunday in May {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Ring ritual: July 30th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Korazusha Annual Festival: August 12th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Reitaisai (Omote Festival): Saturday in the first half of October {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}}: Sunday in the first half of November {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}: Around November 23rd{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Akibasha Annual Festival: December 16th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Emperor's Birthday|Q72318|Tencho Festival}}: December 23rd {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}}* New Year's Eve Festival ({{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}}): December 31st {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Monthly Festival: First half of every month {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} === Precincts === === Sacred tree === It is a large camphor tree with a trunk 8 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. It is said that it was already a large tree during the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}, and is estimated to be 1000 to 1200 years old. In 2000 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}), the Aichi Prefectural Arborist Association conducted treatment{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=23}}. === Office === It was built in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}36 ({{jalink|1903|Q2045}}) as the {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}} It was moved to its current location as a shrine office in July of 1931 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}). It has been certified as a certified regional building asset by Nagoya City {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}. === Setsusha === Currently, there are the following ten subsidiary companies: {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}}. In the past, there were about five other companies. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Current Sessuesha === * Inari Shrine: {{jalink|Uka no Mitama}} Okami{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}} * Korasha: {{jalink|Kenuchi Sukune}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kitano Tenmansha: {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Akibasha: {{jalink|Kikunshuchi}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzosha: {{jalink|Okuninushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Susanoosha: {{jalink|Susanoo}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kotohira Shrine: {{jalink|Oimono Nushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Okuwa Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Toyouke Omikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Korazusha: {{jalink|Chikaji Onna no Mikoto|Q11596349|Chikkana no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Haraido Shrine: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|Seoritsuhime}} God, {{jalink|Kebukito Lord God|Q86734190}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Old Setsuesha === * Kitanosha: {{jalink|Takamisuhigami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzogu: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Doubunsha: {{jalink|Sarutabikojin}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Imori Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Dainichikai Takayuki Spirit}}, {{jalink|Susanoo Mikoto||Susanoo Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Isagami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Gallery === File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 02.jpg|Torii File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 04.jpg|Kitano Tenmansha File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 03.jpg|Korasusha === Festival === In the past, the festival was held throughout the town on the same day as {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}, which is also located in Narumi, but in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink |1700|Q6813}}), a dispute arose between the two shrines, and since then the festival has been divided into front (Narumi Hachiman Shrine) and back (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=34}}. In recent years, there have been efforts to unite the front and back sides and gather floats in Narumi-juku Honmachi, and the festival is now being held as before the festival was divided.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} The Narumi Hachiman Shrine annual festival (Omote Festival) used to be held on August 15th, but since the first year of the Taisho era ({{jalink|1912|Q2077}}) it has been held on {{jalink|September 15th|Q2846}}, and since the Showa era it has been held on {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}. In recent years, the annual festival day has been set on a Saturday in the first half of {{jalink|October|Q124}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}. At the Shōjo Festival, a giant doll called {{jalink|shōjō}} made of a bamboo framework with a red face mask and upper half of the body covered with a costume appears, and there is the ``Narumi Hachiman Shrine Reitaisai Festival'' (Omote Festival), where adults wear shōjō dolls and chase children around. Shōto is the deity of Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. The monkeys hit children with fake hands and sticks, and it is said that children who are hit by the monkeys do not get sick.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}}. === Transportation === * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Nagoya Railway|Q30850|Meitetsu}}{{jalink|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Nagoya Main Line}} {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}}. * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Tokai Railway Company|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|Tokaido Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|Tokaido Main Line}} {{jalink|Odaka Station|Q871424}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=1984|date=1984-11|chapter=|title=History of Midori Ward|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2004|date=2004-03|chapter=|title=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2004}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2005|date=2005-12|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Shrine Journal|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2012|date=2012-05|chapter=|title=Midori Ward local history|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2012}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Toshiyuki Awakawa|year=2013|date=2013-11|chapter=|title=Visiting the history of Midori|publisher=Midori Little History Museum|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Awakawa|2013}}}}* {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2020|date=2020-08|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Local History|publisher=Narumi Tofukai|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2020}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=Kunihiko Sakakibara|year=2021|date=2021-10|chapter=|title=Midori Ward Historic Site Tour|publisher=|isbn=|language=|ref={{Harvid|Sakakibara|2021}}}} === External link === * {{Wayback|url=http://alay.at.webry.info/200610/article_26.html |title=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine |date=20090131165332}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20070930230314/http://www.ne.jp/asahi/no/va/owari/naru_omo/ Narumi Omote Festival (Narumi Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival)] {{shinto-stub}} House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya City|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1155209|Midori Ward}}{{jalink|Narumi Town (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Narumi Town}} Aza Maenowa 23 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|35|4|28|N|136|56|36|E|}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin}}

{{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular festival = Saturday in the first half of October |Alternative name = Hachiman
Narumi Shrine
Bettemiya
Kawahara Shrine |Foundation = unknown (before {{jalink|13th century|Q7049}})|Map = {{Location map|Nagoya|lat_deg=35|lat_min=4|lat_sec=28.1|lon_deg=136|lon_min=56|lon_sec=36.3|label=Narumi Hachimangu Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|4|28.1|N|136|56|36.3|E}}}} }}'''Narumi Hachiman Shrine'' (Narumi Hachimangu) is {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya City|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori Ward (Nagoya City)|Q1155209|Midori Ward}}{{jalink|Narumi Town {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=8}} located in (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Narumi Town}}. {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture)|Q552687|Aichi District}} It is also said to be the Ronsha of ``{{jalink|Kawahara Shrine|Q3194327}}'' and the ronsha of ``Izoe Shrine.'' === Overview === It has been called "Hachiman" since ancient times, and was considered to be a separate shrine to {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}. The location of the shrine was {{jalink|Edago|Q11533553}} in {{jalink|Narumi Village}}, in the area that was called Zennoan Village in the {{jalink|Edo period}}. The year of its founding is unknown, but there are records of shrine occupations dating back to the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, and a {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}} that is over 1,000 years old remains within the precincts, suggesting that it may be an old shrine that dates back to before the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. As mentioned later, it is Shikinaisha's Ronsha. Around this shrine, the remains of a village from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}, ``Maenowa Ruins,'' have been discovered, and {{jalink|Yayoi pottery|Q11487461}} have been unearthed. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} (Shinada Wake no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} (Senagatai Hibi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tamayoribime (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayoribibimei}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Nishi Geishi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} * {{jalink|Tsukuyomi|Q595520|Tsukiyomi}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=3}} === History === The company seems to have already existed in the Kamakura period, and there is a record that Motohisa Oga, the first generation member of the Kuno family, became a company official in the first year of {{jalink|Sadanaga|Q1194168}} ({{jalink|1232|Q5418}}). Since ancient times, it has been called ``Hachimangu Shrine'' and was treated as a companion to Narumi Togu Shrine (Narumi Shrine). {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|During the Warring States period}}, there is a record that {{jalink|Yoshimoto Imagawa|Q1054305}} and {{jalink|Shigemasa Yamaguchi|Q11467507|Chōjiro Shigemasa}} {{jalink|corporate territory}} was {{jalink|donated|Q11455717}} to our company and Narumi Togu Shrine. According to ``{{jalink|Owari Makiyōki}},'' the priest Mr. Kuno submitted a document stating that Narumi Hachimangu Shrine is ``Kawahara Shrine,'' Aichi District, Owari Province, in ``{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}.'' According to ``{{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092}}'', there are some Edo-era fishing lanterns and {{jalink|ridge tags|Q8008834}} that have the words ``Kawahara Shrine'' written on them.On the other hand, there is a theory that it corresponds to "Izoe Shrine" in Aichi District, Owari Province, but according to Kunihiko Sakakibara, a {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Efn|{{jalink|local historian}}, Izoe Shrine was built during the Heian period. When it was abolished in the late period, Atsuta Daiguji disowned Hachimangu Shrine, and during the Oei era ({{jalink|1394|Q6398}} to {{jalink|1428|Q6315}}), the enshrinement site of Izoe Shrine, which had been abolished, was renamed to the old Jizoyama ({{jalink|Narumi Town) (Nagoya City)|Q136913130|Q136913130|Q136913130|Komatsuyama, Narumi Town), it is said to have been rebuilt as a Hachimangu Shrine in the former Tange (Kiyomizu Temple, Narumi Town). It is said that in the early Muromachi period, it was moved from that place to the present-day Azazenowa area, which was at the mouth of the {{jalink|Ogigawa|Q11496839|Kurosuegawa}} large camphor tree. n|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2020|pp=64}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|1984|pp=103}}. Izoe Shrine was later enshrined as Kitanosha, a subsidiary shrine of Hachimangu Shrine, but it is said to have been abolished in the Edo period {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=5}}. }}, from the description in ``{{jalink|Owari Province Fudoki|Q135598023}}'', it is presumed that Izoe Shrine was located in Aichi District {{jalink|Aichi County (Aichi Prefecture) #Go|Q552687|Hibego}}, and it is unreasonable to compare our company with Izoe Shrine.In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}3 ({{jalink|1690|Q7678}}), he served as a priest at Narumi Shrine along with the Makino family.{{jalink| Narumi-juku|Q3033344}}The Kuno family of Honmachi moved to Maenowa and served as a priest.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}), the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests quarreled and the festival was divided. The parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. In recent years, efforts have been seen to bring together the front and back sides of the festival and gather floats at Narumi-juku Honmachi.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} === Chronology === * {{jalink|Koji (Japan)|Q1143910|Koji}} December 3, 17 ({{jalink|1557|Q6595}}) - Yoshimoto Imagawa donates Kanda 11 kan 50 mon to Hachiman Shrine and Narumi Togu (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 17th year ({{jalink|1589|Q6759}}) - Chojiro Shigemasa Yamaguchi donated 20 kanmon of shrine territory to the priests of both Hachimangu and Narumi Togu shrines.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. * {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} August 8 ({{jalink|1622|Q6815}}) - Reconstruction of the main shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink|1700|Q6813}}) - As a result of an argument between the Makino family priests and the Kuno family priests, the parishioners from Sakumachi to Nakajima changed from those of Narumi Shrine to those of Hachimangu Shrine, and the festival began to be held in front and behind the scenes {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=48}}. * {{jalink|Culture|Q11042}} 1st year ({{jalink|1804|Q6894}}) - Constructed a water fountain using trees in the temple grounds {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}}2nd year ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the Chozuya.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}.* Bunsei 12 ({{jalink|1819|Q6967}}) - Rebuild the worship hall, watariden, etc.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}18 ({{jalink|1733|Q6972}}) November 1st - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} Lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachimangu Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=88}}. * August 19, 4th year of {{jalink|Yasunaga}} ({{jalink|1775|Q7700}}) - {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}} lord {{jalink|Muneharu Tokugawa|Q6636871}} visits Narumi Hachiman Shrine {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=34}}. * {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}2nd year ({{jalink|1737|Q6993}}) - Otabisho was built on the banks of the Ogi River{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * {{jalink|Ansei|Q1327259}}6 ({{jalink|1859|Q7687}}) - Torii was rebuilt{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 28, {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) - Promoted to {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Village company}}. The name of the shrine was changed from Hachiman-sha to Hachiman-gu {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}.* Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}) - Construction of the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. *October 26, 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}) - Became a village shrine designated by Heihakuryokushinsha {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=4}}. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}6 ({{jalink|1931|Q18782}}) - Constructing the shrine building {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * April 1934 ({{jalink|1934|Q18714}}) - Built a new inn on the banks of the Ogi River.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * July 1956 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}) - The former {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building was relocated as a company office {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}. * 1969 ({{jalink|1959|Q5302}}) - Damaged by {{jalink|Isewan Typhoon|Q1060156}}, including the worship hall and water purification building.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=15}}. * 1968 ({{jalink|1968|Q2644}}) - The worship hall, main hall, subsidiary shrine, and chozusha were newly built, and the name was changed to Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. * 1973 ({{jalink|1973|Q2477}}) - Transferred Suesha Inarisha from Aiden to Betsuden.{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}* {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}12 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}) - Transferred the subsidiary shrine Kitano Tenmansha from the side hall to the separate hall{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}} === Main annual events === * Saitan Festival: New Year's Day {{jalink|New Year's Day|Q196627|New Year's Day}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Kitano Tenmansha {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}}: Early New Year {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival: February Setsubun Day {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Kigen Festival|Q11604982|Kigen Festival}}: February 11th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}: Sunday in the second half of March {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari}} Festival: Sunday in May {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Ring ritual: July 30th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Korazusha Annual Festival: August 12th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Reitaisai (Omote Festival): Saturday in the first half of October {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Shichi-Go-San|Q1072387}}: Sunday in the first half of November {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}: Around November 23rd{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Akibasha Annual Festival: December 16th {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * {{jalink|Emperor's Birthday|Q72318|Tencho Festival}}: December 23rd {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}}* New Year's Eve Festival ({{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}}): December 31st {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} * Monthly Festival: First half of every month {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=25}} === Precincts === === Sacred tree === It is a large camphor tree with a trunk 8 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=47}}. It is said that it was already a large tree during the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}, and is estimated to be 1000 to 1200 years old. In 2000 ({{jalink|2000|Q1985}}), the Aichi Prefectural Arborist Association conducted treatment{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=23}}. === Office === It was built in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}36 ({{jalink|1903|Q2045}}) as the {{jalink|Narumi Town|Q11675375}} government building. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=14}} It was moved to its current location as a shrine office in July of 1931 ({{jalink|1956|Q5221}}). It has been certified as a certified regional building asset by Nagoya City {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2000|pp=89}}. === Setsusha === Currently, there are the following ten subsidiary companies: {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}}. In the past, there were about five other companies. {{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Current Sessuesha === * Inari Shrine: {{jalink|Uka no Mitama}} Okami{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=11}} * Korasha: {{jalink|Kenuchi Sukune}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kitano Tenmansha: {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Akibasha: {{jalink|Kikunshuchi}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzosha: {{jalink|Okuninushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Susanoosha: {{jalink|Susanoo}} Life{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kotohira Shrine: {{jalink|Oimono Nushikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Okuwa Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Toyouke Omikami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Korazusha: {{jalink|Chikaji Onna no Mikoto|Q11596349|Chikkana no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Haraido Shrine: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|Seoritsuhime}} God, {{jalink|Kebukito Lord God|Q86734190}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Old Setsuesha === * Kitanosha: {{jalink|Takamisuhigami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Kokuzogu: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Doubunsha: {{jalink|Sarutabikojin}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} * Imori Shrine: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Dainichikai Takayuki Spirit}}, {{jalink|Susanoo Mikoto||Susanoo Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Isagami}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2004|pp=12}} === Gallery === File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 02.jpg|Torii File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 04.jpg|Kitano Tenmansha File:Narumi Yahata Shrine 03.jpg|Korasusha === Festival === In the past, the festival was held throughout the town on the same day as {{jalink|Narumi Shrine|Q11495515}}, which is also located in Narumi, but in {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}}13 ({{jalink |1700|Q6813}}), a dispute arose between the two shrines, and since then the festival has been divided into front (Narumi Hachiman Shrine) and back (Narumi Shrine) {{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=34}}. In recent years, there have been efforts to unite the front and back sides and gather floats in Narumi-juku Honmachi, and the festival is now being held as before the festival was divided.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}} The Narumi Hachiman Shrine annual festival (Omote Festival) used to be held on August 15th, but since the first year of the Taisho era ({{jalink|1912|Q2077}}) it has been held on {{jalink|September 15th|Q2846}}, and since the Showa era it has been held on {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}. In recent years, the annual festival day has been set on a Saturday in the first half of {{jalink|October|Q124}}{{Sfn|Sakakibara|2005|pp=26}}. At the Shōjo Festival, a giant doll called {{jalink|shōjō}} made of a bamboo framework with a red face mask and upper half of the body covered with a costume appears, and there is the ``Narumi Hachiman Shrine Reitaisai Festival'' (Omote Festival), where adults wear shōjō dolls and chase children around. Shōto is the deity of Narumi Hachimangu Shrine. The monkeys hit children with fake hands and sticks, and it is said that children who are hit by the monkeys do not get sick.{{Sfn|Awakawa|2013|pp=35}}. === Transportation === * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Nagoya Railway|Q30850|Meitetsu}}{{jalink|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|Nagoya Main Line}} {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}}. * About 20 minutes walk from {{jalink|Tokai Railway Company|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|Tokaido Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|Tokaido Main Line}} {{jalink|Odaka Station|Q871424}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source ===
